title: Bump s3proxy to 3.2.0 (currently pinned at 2.0.0)
summary: rockcraft.yaml pins s3proxy to 2.0.0 in three places (version, source-tag, VERSION build-env). Upstream gaul/s3proxy latest stable is 3.2.0, several minor releases ahead.
why_good_first_issue: Mechanical, well-scoped version bump in a single file with a clear upstream reference; good intro to rockcraft source-tag/build-environment fields.
repro_steps: gh api repos/canonical/s3proxy-rock/contents/rockcraft.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d; gh api repos/gaul/s3proxy/releases/latest --jq .tag_name
repro_result: rockcraft.yaml: version: '2.0.0', source-tag: s3proxy-2.0.0, VERSION: "2.0.0". Upstream releases/latest = s3proxy-3.2.0 (prerelease=false, published 2026-06-15). Tags confirm 3.0.0/3.1.0/3.2.0 exist. Rock is multiple minor versions behind.
fix_hint: Update all three occurrences of 2.0.0 to 3.2.0 (version, source-tag s3proxy-3.2.0, VERSION). Confirm the s3proxy 3.x build still produces target/s3proxy with `mvn package` and that default-jdk JRE is still adequate.
notes: No open issues in repo. Single rockcraft.yaml (no version subdirectories). Repo also uses legacy base syntax 'ubuntu:22.04'.

title: Add goss smoke tests for the s3proxy rock
summary: The repo has no goss.yaml or any smoke tests. There is no automated check that the s3proxy service starts and responds on its S3 endpoint (port 80).
why_good_first_issue: Clear, additive task: create a goss.yaml verifying the s3proxy process runs and the HTTP S3 endpoint responds; mirrors the testing pattern used in sibling COS rocks (e.g. prometheus-rock goss.yaml).
affected_files: new goss.yaml (and wiring into the build/test workflow if present)
repro_result: Tree listing returns no goss.yaml, no tests/, no spread/. Only files are rockcraft.yaml, start-s3proxy.sh, README, LICENSE, SECURITY.md, CODEOWNERS, and .github workflows.
fix_hint: Add goss.yaml with a process check for s3proxy/java and an http check against the S3 endpoint (S3PROXY_ENDPOINT http://0.0.0.0:80, e.g. GET http://localhost:80/ expecting a 403/200 S3-style response).
